settings.title: SETTINGS
settings.subtitle: Customize your experience
settings.deleteAllAlertDesc: Are you sure you want to delete all stickers? This action cannot be undone.
settings.footerSubtitle: v1.0.0 • by Andrey Garcia & Volnei Garcia

metlife.name: New York New Jersey Stadium (MetLife Stadium)
metlife.desc: A versatile venue opened in 2010, this East Rutherford landmark normally hosts the Giants and Jets. In 2026, it transforms for global soccer's biggest event. It previously witnessed Chile's 2016 continental triumph and the 2025 Club World Cup final where Chelsea beat PSG. Legends like Taylor Swift and Ed Sheeran have also filled these stands.

sofi.name: Los Angeles Stadium (SoFi Stadium)
sofi.desc: Inaugurated in late 2020, this cutting-edge arena in Inglewood is a short drive from the historic Rose Bowl. It serves as the home turf for both the Rams and Chargers. Beyond hosting Super Bowl LVI in 2022, its striking architectural roof has covered major concerts featuring stars like BTS and Taylor Swift.

att.name: Dallas Stadium (AT&T Stadium)
att.desc: A masterpiece of modern design located in Arlington, this facility offers spectators an unparalleled view through its massive 25,000-square-foot video screens. Since 2009, it has been the Cowboys' fortress while hosting diverse events from the Gold Cup and NBA All-Star games to major college football playoffs and world-title boxing.

hard_rock.name: Miami Stadium (Hard Rock Stadium)
hard_rock.desc: This multi-purpose hub in Miami Gardens is the primary home of the Dolphins and the prestigious Miami Open tennis matches. It has hosted half a dozen Super Bowls and recently played a key role in the 2025 Club World Cup, including opening matches and Real Madrid's knockout victories. Its versatility extends to Formula 1 racing and major music festivals.

nrg.name: Houston Stadium (NRG Stadium)
nrg.desc: Known for its impressive retractable roof and the energetic 'Bull Pen' fan section, this Houston landmark has a rich soccer history. It has previously welcomed the MLS All-Stars and hosted matches for the 2016 Copa America. While it is the home of the Texans, it also famously hosts the annual Houston Rodeo.

mercedes_benz.name: Atlanta Stadium (Mercedes-Benz Stadium)
mercedes_benz.desc: Defined by its unique pinwheel retractable roof and a massive 360-degree 'halo' screen, this award-winning Atlanta arena is a leader in sustainability. It is the shared home of Atlanta United and the Falcons. Recent highlights include five fixtures during the 2025 Club World Cup, featuring a high-stakes quarter-final match between Paris Saint-Germain and Bayern Munich.

lincoln.name: Philadelphia Stadium (Lincoln Financial Field)
lincoln.desc: Philadelphia’s premier outdoor venue kicked off its history in 2003 with a high-profile friendly between Manchester United and Barcelona. Now the permanent base for the Eagles, it has also hosted the 2015 Gold Cup final. In 2025, it was heavily utilized for eight Club World Cup matches, including Chelsea’s victory on America's Independence Day.

lumen.name: Seattle Stadium (Lumen Field)
lumen.desc: Offering a picturesque view of the Seattle skyline through its open end, this stadium is famously recognized as one of the loudest in sports history. It is the spiritual home of the Sounders and Seahawks. A legendary night here saw the Sounders defeat Pumas in 2022 to claim the continental title, leading to their appearance in the 2025 Club World Cup.

gillette.name: Boston Stadium (Gillette Stadium)
gillette.desc: Following extensive upgrades, this Foxborough stadium now features a mammoth 22,000-square-foot high-definition screen. It serves as the home base for the Revolution and the Patriots. With a history hosting the 2003 Women's World Cup and multiple Gold Cups, it remains a pillar of American sports and a top-tier concert destination.

arrowhead.name: Kansas City Stadium (Arrowhead Stadium)
arrowhead.desc: Holding the world record for noise levels at an outdoor venue, this Kansas City landmark is the legendary home of the Chiefs. Known globally as a soccer stronghold, the city supports both Sporting KC and the KC Current. The atmosphere here is unrivaled, having hosted five straight NFL conference championships recently.

levis.name: San Francisco Bay Area Stadium (Levi's Stadium)
levis.desc: Located in Santa Clara, this modern facility has been the 49ers' home since 2014. It famously hosted Super Bowl 50 and served as the venue for Super Bowl LX in early 2026. Beyond football, it has staged high-profile events like the Copa America opening match and Wrestlemania.

azteca.name: Mexico City Stadium (Estadio Azteca)
azteca.desc: A legendary cathedral of the sport, this iconic Mexico City venue is the first to host three separate World Cups. It witnessed the genius of Pele and Maradona in the 1970 and 1986 finals. Currently hosting Club America and the Mexican national team, it also set attendance records during the 2011 U-17 World Cup final.

bbva.name: Estadio Monterrey (Estadio BBVA)
bbva.desc: This eco-friendly architectural gem in Monterrey was the first in the region to achieve LEED silver certification. Inaugurated in 2015, it serves as the home for CF Monterrey. It recently hosted the 2022 Concacaf W Championship final and has welcomed international music stars like Bad Bunny and Justin Bieber.

akron.name: Estadio Guadalajara (Estadio Akron)
akron.desc: Known for its striking spherical shape and grass-covered exterior, this Zapopan arena is one of the most visual venues in Mexico. Since 2010, it has been the home of Chivas, celebrating multiple league and continental titles. It also played a central role in the 2011 U-17 World Cup and the Pan-American Games.

toronto.name: Toronto Stadium (BMO Field)
toronto.desc: Originally constructed for the 2007 U-20 World Cup, this venue saw Argentina take the trophy with a young Sergio Aguero. It features a distinct canopy and has become the heart of Canadian soccer, especially during the successful 2022 qualification run. It also hosted the U-20 Women's World Cup in 2014.

bc_place.name: BC Place Vancouver (BC Place)
bc_place.desc: Overlooking Vancouver's False Creek, this renovated architectural icon has anchored the city's sports scene for decades. Home to the Whitecaps and BC Lions, its most famous moment was hosting the 2015 Women's World Cup final, where a historic hat-trick secured the title for the United States.

stickerModal.question1: How many <1>repeated</1> stickers do you have?

trade.title: Trade Center
trade.subtitle: Share or Scan Stickers
trade.code_desc: Show this QR Code to a friend to scan.
trade.update_notice: Don't forget to update your duplicates if you make a trade.
trade.matches_found: Great news! He has {{count}} sticker(s) that you don't have!
trade.no_matches: Too bad, he doesn't have any stickers you need right now.
trade.cam_permission: We need camera permission to scan.
